---
title: "Introduction to Docker"
author: "Sanduni Rajapaksa"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, utils/custom.scss]
    css: utils/custom.css
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: false
    incremental: false
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    toc: false
    center: false
    smaller: true
    preview-links: true
    overview: true
    controls: true
    progress: true
    scrollable: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    fig-align: center
    width: 1440
    height: 810
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Introduction to Docker {.nonincremental}

### Workshop Overview

- Build a practical understanding of Docker containers and images.
- Learn to run, inspect, and manage containers from the command line.
- Create your own container images with Dockerfiles.
- Apply container workflows to research reproducibility.

### Why This Workshop?

- Portable environments across macOS, Linux, and Windows.
- Reduced setup friction and fewer dependency conflicts.
- Better collaboration and reproducibility for computational work.

## Prerequisites

- Basic familiarity with using a command shell.
- Ability to create and edit files with a text editor.
- Docker installed and running on your machine.
- A Docker Hub account for image pull/push exercises.

## Workshop Structure

- Episode 1: Introducing Containers
- Episode 2: Introducing the Docker Command Line
- Episode 3: Exploring and Running Containers
- Episode 4: Cleaning Up Containers
- Episode 5: Finding Containers on Docker Hub
- Episode 6: Creating Your Own Container Images
- Episode 7: Creating More Complex Container Images
- Episode 8: Examples of Using Container Images in Practice
- Episode 9: Containers in Research Workflows: Reproducibility and Granularity

{{< include 01-introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include 02-meet-docker.qmd >}}

{{< include 03-running-containers.qmd >}}

{{< include 04-managing-containers.qmd >}}

{{< include 05-docker-hub.qmd >}}

{{< include 06-creating-container-images.qmd >}}

{{< include 07-advanced-containers.qmd >}}

{{< include 08-docker-image-examples.qmd >}}

{{< include 09-reproduciblity.qmd >}}


## Thank You

Questions and discussion.
